methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $2.7B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 10 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $7.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.2x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: SambaNova Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $5B

data.narrative:
Tenstorrent, a Series D chip design company, is evaluated against a universe of 10 private and 3 public semiconductor peers. The target's last post-money valuation stands at $2.7B following $1B+ in total capital raised.

Two valuation methods apply to this analysis. Stage-progression methodology, applying a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D advancement, yields $4.0B. The peer-median approach across 10 same-sector comparables with similar valuation tiers produces $2.2B, with the peer set ranging from $1.0B to $7.0B (including SambaNova Systems at $5.0B, PsiQuantum at $7.0B, and Rebellions at $2.3B). Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of revenue disclosure—standard for private companies. The public semiconductor median P/S of 24.2x across ALAB, NVDA, and AMD remains inapplicable without target revenue.

Triangulating the two applicable methods yields a valuation range of $1.8B to $4.7B, with a median of $3.1B. The current $2.7B valuation positions within this range but toward the lower bound, suggesting modest upside to the median. Key uncertainties include product-stage maturity relative to SambaNova and Cerebras, actual revenue run-rate, and near-term capital needs. The wide peer dispersion ($1.0B–$7.0B) reflects genuine heterogeneity in chip-sector risk profiles and commercialization timelines.
